facts: An exit load is a fee charged by the Asset Management Company (AMC), not INDMoney, when you redeem mutual fund units before a specified period.
INDMoney charges zero platform fees, zero commissions, and zero withdrawal charges for mutual fund transactions.
For most Equity Mutual Funds, the exit load is typically 1% if units are redeemed within 12 months (365 days) of investment.
Debt funds and Liquid funds have much shorter exit load periods, often ranging from 0 to 7 days, depending on the specific fund scheme.
The exit load is calculated as a percentage of the Net Asset Value (NAV) at the time of redemption and is deducted from the final payout.
The 'First-In, First-Out' (FIFO) rule applies for redemptions, meaning units bought first are considered redeemed first for exit load calculations.
